chunk_summary: This section provides a one-page prescription template for nutrition management in chronic kidney and liver disease, with India-specific food swaps. It emphasizes sodium reduction and dietary adjustments based on clinical guidelines and local food composition data.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of sodium reduction in managing chronic kidney and liver disease.
Learn how to implement India-adapted food swaps in dietary prescriptions.
Familiarize with clinical guidelines for pediatric nutrition in CKD and liver disease.
Utilize Indian Food Composition Tables for culturally appropriate dietary planning.
key_facts: Sodium intake should be reduced to less than 2 grams per day, as per WHO guidelines.
The Indian Food Composition Tables (2017) provide region-specific nutritional data for dietary planning.
The Pediatric Renal Nutrition Taskforce recommends tailored sodium management for children with CKD stages 2–5 and on dialysis.
NASPGHAN and ESPGHAN emphasize the importance of nutrition support in children with chronic liver disease.
The KDOQI guidelines (2008) provide evidence-based recommendations for pediatric CKD nutrition management.
reference_quote: "Sodium intake should be reduced to less than 2 grams per day to mitigate risks associated with chronic kidney and liver disease." (Source: WHO fact sheet on sodium reduction)
document_key_topics: Sodium reduction in CKD and liver disease
India-adapted food swaps for dietary management
Pediatric nutrition guidelines for CKD and liver disease
Use of Indian Food Composition Tables in clinical practice
